scenario_results.reason: Privileged-access review guidance stayed inside walkthrough, population, sampling, evidence, and exception logic without drifting into IAM remediation.
Procure-to-pay evidence review focused on sufficiency, precision, period coverage, and lineage rather than procurement execution.
Licensing response tested approvals and review evidence while routing entitlement optimization and negotiation outside the boundary.
Cloud billing control review assessed variance-investigation evidence and approval steps without claiming FinOps ownership.
Change-management response defined sample and re-performance logic without executing platform administration.
SOC reliance guidance identified complementary customer controls and evidence dependencies without implying audit sign-off.
Deficiency analysis weighed repeated exceptions and compensating-control weakness using risk-based prioritization.
Traceability review required reproducible linkage from walkthrough to conclusion and explicitly flagged missing lineage.
Legal and accounting conclusions were rejected clearly with correct escalation.
ERP remediation and vendor negotiation were refused while preserving only controls-testing observations.
Low-confidence case requested minimum population and evidence artifacts instead of inventing a test conclusion.
Fallback handling preserved the local reviewed baseline and avoided unsupported commercialization or source-freshness claims.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against issue #125 requirements, including policy and control analysis, commercial and licensing control reasoning, evidence and traceability quality, risk-based prioritization, low-confidence escalation, out-of-scope rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification is recorded in readiness evidence.
